operating_state: American Tower generated $11.1 billion in total revenue for 2023, up from $10.7 billion in 2022, with 99% from property operations across 224,502 communications sites globally and 28 data centers. International segments delivered mixed performance with growth in Europe and Asia-Pacific offset by Latin America pressures. The company experienced approximately 3% tenant churn primarily from T-Mobile legacy Sprint lease cancellations expected to continue through 2025, while VIL in India resumed full monthly payments in the second half after partial payment periods.
cost_structure: Property operations maintained segment gross margin of 71% of total revenue, driven by minimal incremental costs for adding tenants to existing sites. Preliminary asset useful life reviews suggest tower lives may extend beyond current 20-year estimates, potentially reducing 2024 depreciation by $700-800 million from the 2023 level of $1.5 billion.
material_events: AMT signed an agreement in January 2024 to sell its India operations to Data Infrastructure Trust for approximately $2.5 billion, expected to close in second half 2024 subject to regulatory approvals. The company recorded $724 million in total impairment charges including goodwill impairments for India and Spain reporting units plus asset impairments primarily in Africa. Management completed $5.7 billion in senior note issuances and a $1.3 billion securitization transaction while disposing of Mexico Fiber and Poland operations.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted revenue by $296 million with 44% of revenues and 53% of operating expenses denominated in foreign currencies, creating significant translation risk from $8.3 billion EUR debt exposure. The company recorded $331 million foreign currency losses compared to $449 million gains in the prior year due to exchange rate fluctuations on intercompany balances. Ghana operations adopted highly inflationary accounting effective January 2024.
forward_outlook: Management expects over $60 billion in non-cancellable tenant lease revenue from existing contracts with contractual rent escalations averaging 3% annually in the United States. The company anticipates continued organic growth from 5G deployments and network densification requirements, with planned 2024 capital expenditures of $1.5-1.7 billion. Proceeds from the pending India transaction will be used to repay existing credit facility borrowings.
uncertainty_profile: The India transaction closing remains subject to government and regulatory approvals with no certainty of completion timing, while VIL customer relationship continues to present collection risk despite payment normalization. Asset retirement obligations carry $4.0 billion in estimated undiscounted future cash outlay. Debt covenant compliance requires maintaining total leverage below 6.0x and senior secured leverage below 3.0x EBITDA ratios.
